# 🎯 Task 02.2: N-gram Tokenizer

## 📅 Sprint Info
- **Duration:** 3 days
- **Milestone:** M1 - Core Retrieval
- **Priority:** P0 (Critical Path)
- **Depends On:** Task 01.6 (C++ Integration) ✅
- **Blocks:** Task 02.3 (Index Builder)
- **Assignee:** TBD

## 🎬 What You'll Build
Character-level n-gram tokenizer (3-5 grams) for fallback retrieval. Handles unknown/mixed language content.

## 📋 Daily Breakdown

### Day 1: N-gram Generation
- [ ] Implement character 3-gram generation
- [ ] Implement character 4-gram generation
- [ ] Implement character 5-gram generation
- [ ] Edge case handling (short text)
- [ ] Basic unit tests

### Day 2: Optimization & Unicode
- [ ] Unicode-aware n-gram generation
- [ ] Memory-efficient implementation
- [ ] Sliding window optimization
- [ ] Test with CJK, Arabic, mixed scripts

### Day 3: Integration & Testing
- [ ] Integration with BM25 scorer
- [ ] Performance benchmarking
- [ ] Comprehensive test suite
- [ ] Documentation

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Generates 3-5 character n-grams
- [ ] Unicode-aware (works for all scripts)
- [ ] Memory efficient for large documents
- [ ] Performance: 10K+ docs/sec
- [ ] Improves recall for mixed-language by ≥20%

## 🎉 Celebration Criteria
✅ **Demo:** Match "searhc" → "search" using n-grams
✅ **Universal:** Works for unknown languages  

**🎊 Celebration:** Find typos automatically!

## 📦 Deliverables
- `src/search/NGramTokenizer.cpp` (200 lines)
- `include/search/NGramTokenizer.h` (80 lines)
- `tests/ngram_test.cpp` (100+ cases)

## 🚀 Next Steps
➡️ **Task 02.3: Index Builder** (needs both BM25 and n-grams)

**Handle every language and typo! 🔤**
